Im Gegenzug lassen wir unsere Leute ihre Karriere besitzen. Ihre Ideen, groß und klein, wirklich verbessern die Welt. Und das ist die Art von Arbeit, von der wir teilnehmen wollen. Â Mansioni - Durchführen von End-to-End Drug Product Manufacturing unter GMP: Chargenausführung, Dokumentation, Fehlersuche und Überprüfung der Ergebnisse. - Ergänzen und pflegen Sie alle GMP-Dokumente (Batch Records, Logbücher, Formulare). Prozessänderungen und CAPA-Aktivitäten unterstützen. - Vergewissern Sie sich, dass jede Charge sicher, pünktlich und nach Qualität und aseptischen Standards hergestellt wird. - Einrichtung, Betrieb und saubere Ausrüstung für flüssige und gefriergetrocknete (lyophilisierte) Fläschchenherstellung. - Unterstützung von Compounding & Formulierungen, Pufferpräparation, Waschen/Autoklavieren, Filterintegritätsprüfung, Füllen/Beschreiben und visuelle Inspektion. - Trainieren Sie Kollegen und unterstützen Sie funktionsübergreifende Teams mit technischer Anleitung. - Reportieren Sie Geräteprobleme und unterstützen Sie Engineering-Teams mit Reparaturen in Reinraum/aseptischen Bereichen. Requisiti - 2–4 Jahre GMP Shopfloor Erfahrung (ideal sterile Drug Product / Fill & Finish). - Drug Product Erfahrung ist ein starker Vorteil. EFZ Ausbildung (mechanisch oder ähnlich: Produktionsmechanik, Automechanik, etc.) Auch praktische Erfahrung in Berufen mit hohen Hygiene- und Sauberkeitsanforderungen wie Koch/Koch oder vergleichbaren Rollen. - Gute Englischkenntnisse für Anweisungen und Kommunikation; Deutsch ist ein Plus. - Vertrautheit mit GMP, SOPs und Qualitätsprozessen. - Zuverlässiger, gut organisierter, teamorientierter Arbeitsstil. - IT-Kenntnisse (SAP/MES ist ein Plus). - Bereitschaft, die On-Call-Zölle in gewissem Maße zu unterstützen. - Derzeit in einem 2‐Schicht-System; Bereitschaft für 3-Schicht-Arbeit (regelmäßig 5-Tage-Woche, gelegentlicher Samstag, keine Sonntage). Cosa offriamo - Eine agile Karriere und dynamische Arbeitskultur. - Ja. Ein inklusiver und ethischer Arbeitsplatz. - Kompensationsprogramme, die hohe Leistung erkennen. - Eine Vielzahl von Vorteilen hängen von Rolle und Standort ab. Die vollständige Liste unserer weltweiten Vorteile finden Sie auch unter https://www.lonza.com/careers/benefits. Contatto Jeden Tag haben die Produkte und Dienstleistungen von Lonza positive Auswirkungen auf Millionen von Menschen. Für uns ist dies nicht nur ein großes Privileg, sondern auch eine große Verantwortung. Wie wir unsere Geschäftsergebnisse erzielen, ist ebenso wichtig wie die Leistungen selbst. In Lonza respektieren und schützen wir unser Volk und unsere Umwelt. Jeder Erfolg, den wir erreichen, ist überhaupt kein Erfolg, wenn nicht ethisch erreicht. Die Menschen kommen nach Lonza für die Herausforderung und Kreativität, komplexe Probleme zu lösen und neue Ideen in den Life Sciences zu entwickeln. Im Gegenzug bieten wir die Zufriedenheit, die mit der Verbesserung des Lebens auf der ganzen Welt kommt. Die Zufriedenheit, die mit einem sinnvollen Unterschied kommt. Lonza ist ein Arbeitgeber der Chancengleichheit. Alle qualifizierten Bewerber erhalten eine Berücksichtigung für die Beschäftigung ohne Bezug auf Rasse, Religion, Farbe, nationale Herkunft, Sex, sexuelle Orientierung, Geschlechtsidentität, Alter, Status als qualifiziertes Individuum mit Behinderung, geschützter Veteran-Status oder andere durch Gesetz geschützte Merkmale. Lonza ist weltweit führend in der Pharma- und Biotech-Produktion. Das Unternehmen betreibt große Produktionsstätten in Visp (Valais), Basel und Stein (Aargau), Schweiz.
descriptionByLocale.fr: Descrizione Emplacement. Vispe. Aujourd'hui, Lonza est un leader mondial des sciences de la vie sur trois continents. Pendant que nous travaillons dans la science, il n'y a pas de formule magique pour comment nous le faisons. Notre plus grande solution scientifique est de travailler ensemble avec des gens talentueux, en concevant des idées qui aident les entreprises à aider les gens. En échange, nous laissons notre peuple posséder sa carrière. Leurs idées, grandes et petites, améliorent véritablement le monde. Et c'est le genre de travail dont nous voulons faire partie. ## Mansioni - Effectuer la fabrication de bout en bout de produits pharmaceutiques sous BPF : exécution par lots, documentation, dépannage et examen des résultats. - Remplir et tenir à jour toute la documentation sur les BPF (enregistrements de lots, journaux de bord, formulaires). Changements au processus de soutien et activités de l'ACAP. - S'assurer que chaque lot est fabriqué en toute sécurité, à temps et selon les normes de qualité et d'aseptique. - Mettre en place, utiliser et nettoyer des équipements pour la fabrication de flacons liquides et lyophilisés. - Prise en charge du composé et des formulations, préparation tampon, lavage/autoclavement, contrôle de l'intégrité du filtre, remplissage/crimage et inspection visuelle. - Former des collègues et soutenir des équipes interfonctionnelles avec des conseils techniques. - Signaler les problèmes d'équipement et soutenir les équipes d'ingénierie avec des réparations dans les zones propres / aseptiques. ## Requisiti - 2–4 ans d'expérience sur les BPF (idéalement stérile produit pharmaceutique / remplissage et finition). - L'expérience du produit pharmaceutique est un avantage important. Apprentissage EFZ (mécanicien ou similaire: mécanicien de production, mécanicien automobile, etc.) Expérience pratique également dans des professions exigeant une hygiène et une propreté élevées, comme le cuisinier/chef ou des rôles comparables. - Bonnes compétences en anglais pour les instructions et la communication ; l'allemand est un plus. - Connaissance des BPF, des SOP et des processus de qualité. - Style de travail fiable, bien organisé et axé sur l'équipe. - Connaissances informatiques (SAP/MES est un plus). - Volonté de soutenir les fonctions de garde dans une certaine mesure. - Fonctionnant actuellement dans un système de 2 postes; préparation au travail en 3 postes (semaine régulière de 5 jours, samedi occasionnel, pas de dimanche). ## Cosa offriamo - Une carrière agile et une culture de travail dynamique. - Oui. Un milieu de travail inclusif et éthique. - Les programmes de rémunération qui reconnaissent le rendement élevé. - Une variété de prestations dépendant du rôle et de l'emplacement. La liste complète de nos avantages globaux peut également être consultée à l'adresse https://www.lonza.com/careers/ benefits. ## Contatto Chaque jour, les produits et services de Lonza ont un impact positif sur des millions de personnes. Pour nous, ce n'est pas seulement un grand privilège, mais aussi une grande responsabilité. La façon dont nous obtenons nos résultats commerciaux est tout aussi importante que les réalisations elles-mêmes. À Lonza, nous respectons et protégeons notre peuple et notre environnement. Tout succès que nous obtenons n'est pas du tout un succès sinon un succès éthique. Les gens viennent à Lonza pour le défi et la créativité de résoudre des problèmes complexes et de développer de nouvelles idées dans les sciences de la vie. En retour, nous offrons la satisfaction qui vient avec l'amélioration de la vie partout dans le monde. La satisfaction qui vient avec faire une différence significative. Lonza est un employeur à chances égales. Tous les candidats qualifiés seront considérés pour un emploi sans égard à la race, à la religion, à la couleur, à l'origine nationale, au sexe, à l'orientation sexuelle, à l'identité de genre, à l'âge, au statut d'individu qualifié handicapé, au statut d'ancien combattant protégé ou à toute autre caractéristique protégée par la loi. Lonza est un leader mondial dans la fabrication de produits pharmaceutiques et de biotechnologies. La société exploite d'importantes installations de production à Viège (Valais), Bâle et Stein (Argovie), Suisse.

jobs.title: Manager, MSAT Process Lead
jobs.description:
After an onboarding period in Visp, Switzerland, the position will be based in the U.S. (East or Mid-West Coast) to support the expansion of small molecules/bioconjugation capabilities. Relocation targeted in Q1 2027 and assistance is available for eligible candidates and their families, if needed. 

 

What you will get: 

An agile career and dynamic working culture, with the opportunity to work on an international assignment

A highly collaborative environment that fosters teamwork and inclusion 

A range of professional development programs to grow your skills and career 

An ethical workplace where tasks are carried out in accordance with relevant procedures 

The full list of our global benefits can be also found on https://www.lonza.com/careers/benefits. 

 

What you will do: 

As the MSAT Process Lead, you will define and drive process technology strategy for a new pharmaceutical manufacturing facility. You will play a key role in facility design, technology selection, and startup, ensuring scalable, efficient, and GMP-compliant manufacturing processes. 

Define and support process technology strategy aligned with product and facility needs 

Lead selection and implementation of process equipment and process analytical technology (PAT) 

Collaborate with internal teams and customers to understand product requirements, including critical quality attributes (CQAs), process parameters, and scale-up considerations 

Develop production models (throughput, cycle times, capacity) to support facility design and operational planning 

Evaluate and recommend technology solutions, balancing performance, cost, flexibility, and compliance  

Provide technical input into user requirement specifications (URS), process flow diagrams, and equipment layouts 

Partner with automation and OT teams to integrate process control and PAT strategies 

Support commissioning and qualification activities, including FAT and SAT 

Interface with stakeholders throughout design, construction, and startup phases 

 

What we are looking for: 

Bachelor’s degree in chemical engineering, Bioprocess Engineering, or related field required; advanced degree preferred 

8+ years of experience in MSAT, process engineering, or technical operations within a GMP-regulated pharmaceutical or biotech environment 

Strong expertise in process equipment, PAT, and manufacturing technologies 

Experience in bioconjugates and/or advanced synthesis (e.g., complex biologics, ADCs, or small molecule processes) 

Proven ability to translate product requirements into scalable manufacturing solutions 

Experience supporting facility startup, commissioning, and tech transfer 

Strong analytical and problem-solving skills, including production modeling and process evaluation 

Familiarity with automation systems, process control strategies, and data integrity principles 

Excellent communication and stakeholder management skills, including customer-facing experience 

Collaborative mindset with the ability to work across functions and influence decision-making 

Travel: Willingness and ability to travel to EMEA and NA to meet business objectives 

 

About Lonza 

At Lonza, our people are our greatest strength. With 30+ sites across five continents, our globally connected teams work together every day to manufacture the medicines of tomorrow. Our core values of Collaboration, Accountability, Excellence, Passion and Integrity reflect who we are and how we work together. Everyone’s ideas, big or small, have the potential to improve millions of lives, and that’s the kind of work we want you to be part of. 

Innovation thrives when people from all backgrounds bring their unique perspectives to the table. At Lonza, we offer equal employment opportunities to all qualified applicants regardless of race, religion, color, national origin, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, age, disability, veteran status, or any other characteristic protected by law. If you’re ready to help turn our customers’ breakthrough ideas into viable therapies, we look forward to welcoming you on board. 

Ready to shape the future of life sciences? Apply now.

Lonza is a global leader in pharma and biotech manufacturing. The company operates major production facilities in Visp (Valais), Basel, and Stein (Aargau), Switzerland.

descriptionByLocale.it: Descrizione Dopo un periodo di imbarco a Visp, in Svizzera, la posizione si baserà negli Stati Uniti (Costa Est o Mid-Ovest) per sostenere l'espansione di piccole molecole / capacità di bioconiugazione. Ripartizione mirata nel Q1 2027 e l'assistenza è disponibile per i candidati idonei e le loro famiglie, se necessario. ## Mansioni - # Come MSAT Process Lead, definire e guidare la strategia di tecnologia di processo per un nuovo impianto di produzione farmaceutica. - No. Potrai svolgere un ruolo chiave nella progettazione delle strutture, nella selezione della tecnologia e nell'avvio, garantendo processi di produzione scalabili, efficienti e conformi a GMP. - Definire e sostenere la strategia di processo allineata alle esigenze di prodotto e struttura - Lead selezione e implementazione di apparecchiature di processo e tecnologia analitica di processo (PAT) - Collaborare con team interni e clienti per comprendere i requisiti di prodotto, tra cui attributi di qualità critica (CQAs), parametri di processo e considerazioni di scala-up - Sviluppare modelli di produzione (tramite, tempi di ciclo, capacità) per supportare la progettazione e la pianificazione operativa Una carriera agile e una cultura del lavoro dinamica, con l'opportunità di lavorare su un incarico internazionale - Un ambiente altamente collaborativo che favorisce il lavoro di squadra e l'inclusione - Una gamma di programmi di sviluppo professionale per crescere le vostre competenze e carriera - Un luogo di lavoro etico in cui i compiti sono svolti secondo le procedure pertinenti - L'elenco completo dei nostri benefici globali è disponibile anche su https://www.lonza.com/careers/benefits ## About Lonza At Lonza, la nostra gente è la nostra forza. Con 30+ siti in cinque continenti, i nostri team collegati a livello globale lavorano insieme ogni giorno per produrre i farmaci di domani. I nostri valori fondamentali di collaborazione, responsabilità, eccellenza, passione e integrità riflettono chi siamo e come lavoriamo insieme. Le idee di tutti, grandi o piccole, hanno il potenziale di migliorare milioni di vite, e questo è il tipo di lavoro di cui vogliamo che tu faccia parte. L'innovazione prospera quando le persone di tutti gli sfondi portano le loro prospettive uniche al tavolo. A Lonza offriamo pari opportunità di lavoro a tutti i candidati qualificati indipendentemente da razza, religione, colore, origine nazionale, sesso, orientamento sessuale, identità di genere, età, disabilità, status veterano, o qualsiasi altra caratteristica protetta dalla legge. Se siete pronti ad aiutare a trasformare le idee innovative dei nostri clienti in terapie valide, non vediamo l'ora di accogliervi a bordo. ## Contatto Pronto a plasmare il futuro delle scienze della vita? Applichi subito. Lonza è leader globale nella produzione di farmaci e biotecnologie. L'azienda gestisce importanti stabilimenti produttivi a Visp (Valais), Basilea e Stein (Aargau), Svizzera.

Wenn Sie bereit sind, zu helfen, die bahnbrechenden Ideen unserer Kunden in lebensfähige Therapien zu verwandeln, freuen wir uns, Sie an Bord zu begrüßen. descriptionByLocale.fr: Descrizione Après une période d'embarquement à Viège, en Suisse, la position sera basée aux États-Unis (Est ou Mid-West Coast) pour soutenir l'expansion de petites molécules/capacités de bioconjugaison. La réinstallation visée au premier trimestre 2027 et l'aide sont disponibles pour les candidats admissibles et leurs familles, au besoin. - Oui. En tant que responsable du processus MSAT, vous définirez et piloterez la stratégie technologique du processus pour une nouvelle installation de fabrication pharmaceutique. - Oui. Vous jouerez un rôle clé dans la conception des installations, la sélection des technologies et le démarrage, assurant des processus de fabrication évolutifs, efficaces et conformes aux BPF. - Définir et appuyer la stratégie technologique du processus en fonction des besoins du produit et de l'installation - Choisir et mettre en oeuvre l'équipement du processus et la technologie d'analyse du processus (TAP) - Collaborer avec les équipes internes et les clients pour comprendre les exigences du produit, y compris les attributs de qualité critiques (AQC), les paramètres du processus et les considérations relatives à l'échelle - Élaborer des modèles de production (performance, durée du cycle, capacité) pour appuyer la conception et la planification opérationnelle de l'installation - Évaluer et recommander des solutions technologiques, équilibrer les performances, les coûts, la flexibilité et la conformité - Fournir une contribution technique aux spécifications des exigences de l'utilisateur (URS), aux diagrammes de flux de processus et à la configuration de l'équipement - Partenariat avec les équipes d'automatisation et d'OT pour intégrer le contrôle du processus et les stratégies de PAT - Soutenir les activités de mise en service et de qualification, y compris la FAT et la SAT - Interface avec les intervenants tout au long de la conception, de la construction et du démarrage ## Requisiti - 8+ ans d'expérience en matière de MSAT, d'ingénierie du processus ou d'exploitation Une carrière agile et une culture de travail dynamique, avec l'occasion de travailler sur une affectation internationale - Un environnement hautement collaboratif qui favorise le travail d'équipe et l'inclusion - Une gamme de programmes de perfectionnement professionnel pour développer vos compétences et votre carrière - Un lieu de travail éthique où les tâches sont exécutées conformément aux procédures pertinentes - La liste complète de nos avantages globaux peut également être consultée à l'adresse https://www.lonza.com/careers/ benefits À propos de Lonza Chez Lonza, notre peuple est notre plus grande force. Avec plus de 30 sites sur cinq continents, nos équipes connectées à l'échelle mondiale travaillent tous les jours pour fabriquer les médicaments de demain. Nos valeurs fondamentales de collaboration, de responsabilisation, d'excellence, de passion et d'intégrité reflètent qui nous sommes et comment nous travaillons ensemble. Les idées de tout le monde, grandes ou petites, ont le potentiel d'améliorer des millions de vies, et c'est le genre de travail auquel nous voulons que vous participiez. L'innovation prospère lorsque les gens de tous les horizons apportent leurs points de vue uniques à la table. À Lonza, nous offrons des possibilités d'emploi égales à tous les candidats qualifiés indépendamment de la race, de la religion, de la couleur, de l'origine nationale, du sexe, de l'orientation sexuelle, de l'identité de genre, de l'âge, du handicap, du statut de vétéran ou de toute autre caractéristique protégée par la loi. Si vous êtes prêt à aider nos clients à transformer des idées révolutionnaires en thérapies viables, nous nous réjouissons de vous accueillir à bord. ## Contatto Prêt à façonner l'avenir des sciences de la vie? Demande maintenant. Lonza est un leader mondial dans la fabrication de produits pharmaceutiques et de biotechnologies. La société exploite d'importantes installations de production à Viège (Valais), Bâle et Stein (Argovie), Suisse.

jobs.title: Manufacturing Process Expert Lead

titleByLocale.fr: Expert en procédés de fabrication plomb

jobs.description:
After an onboarding period in Visp, Switzerland, the position will be based in the U.S. (East or Mid-West Coast) to support the expansion of small molecules/bioconjugation capabilities. Relocation targeted in Q1 2027 and assistance is available for eligible candidates and their families, if needed. 

 

In this role, you will have a direct impact by optimizing daily GMP operations, improving process robustness, and ensuring safe, compliant, and timely production. You will also play a key role in capability expansion—supporting equipment introductions, authoring critical documentation, and resolving issues through data driven problem solving and cross functional collaboration. 

 

What you will get: 

An agile career and dynamic working culture, with the opportunity to work on an international assignment

A highly collaborative environment that fosters teamwork and inclusion 

A range of professional development programs to grow your skills and career 

An ethical workplace where tasks are carried out in accordance with relevant procedures 

The full list of our global benefits can be also found on https://www.lonza.com/careers/benefits. 

 

 

What you will do: 

Lead the day-to-day execution of manufacturing activities in a GMP environment, helping ensure safe, reliable, and high-quality operations. 

Partner closely with cross-functional teams to support the introduction of new equipment and capabilities, including user requirements input and participation in key project milestones such as FAT and SAT. 

Prepare manufacturing areas for successful operations by updating documentation, ensuring equipment readiness, and performing standard checks before batch activities. 

Provide guidance and on-the-job training to colleagues on new processes, safety practices, and good documentation behaviors, helping foster a culture of learning and team development. 

Support troubleshooting and problem-solving with engineering, quality, and technical experts to keep operations running smoothly and implement effective improvements. 

Use practical continuous-improvement tools to simplify processes, enhance efficiency, and strengthen overall performance of equipment and workflows. 

Represent the manufacturing area confidently during audits, technical transfers, and early production campaigns linked to newly introduced technologies. 

 

What we are looking for: 

Bachelor’s degree in a scientific or engineering field—or equivalent relevant experience and learning gained through industry roles. 

Experience working in GMP manufacturing, pharmaceutical operations, biotechnology, or a similar regulated environment. 

Experience in bioconjugates and/or advanced synthesis (e.g., complex biologics, ADCs, or small molecule processes) 

Ability to contribute to equipment and process introduction activities, with familiarity in areas such as FAT/SAT and qualification steps considered beneficial. 

Clear and supportive communication skills, with the ability to train and collaborate with colleagues across functions and levels. 

A practical approach to problem-solving, using structured methods to identify root causes and drive improvements. 

A proactive, organized mindset, with the ability to balance multiple activities while maintaining a commitment to safety, quality, and continuous improvement. 

Travel: Willingness and ability to travel to EMEA and NA to meet business objectives

 

About Lonza 

 

At Lonza, our people are our greatest strength. With 30+ sites across five continents, our globally connected teams work together every day to manufacture the medicines of tomorrow. Our core values of Collaboration, Accountability, Excellence, Passion and Integrity reflect who we are and how we work together. Everyone’s ideas, big or small, have the potential to improve millions of lives, and that’s the kind of work we want you to be part of. 

 

Innovation thrives when people from all backgrounds bring their unique perspectives to the table. At Lonza, we value diversity and are committed to creating an inclusive environment for all employees. If you are ready to help turn our customers’ breakthrough ideas into viable therapies, we look forward to welcoming you on board. 

 

Ready to shape the future of life sciences? Apply now.

Lonza is a global leader in pharma and biotech manufacturing. The company operates major production facilities in Visp (Valais), Basel, and Stein (Aargau), Switzerland.

descriptionByLocale.it: Descrizione Dopo un periodo di imbarco a Visp, in Svizzera, la posizione si baserà negli Stati Uniti (Costa Est o Mid-Ovest) per sostenere l'espansione di piccole molecole / capacità di bioconiugazione. Ripartizione mirata nel Q1 2027 e l'assistenza è disponibile per i candidati idonei e le loro famiglie, se necessario. ## Mansioni - Condurre l'esecuzione quotidiana delle attività produttive in un ambiente GMP, contribuendo a garantire operazioni sicure, affidabili e di alta qualità. - Collaborare a stretto contatto con i team interfunzionali per supportare l'introduzione di nuove attrezzature e funzionalità, tra cui l'ingresso dei requisiti utente e la partecipazione a milestone di progetto chiave come FAT e SAT. - Preparare le aree di produzione per operazioni di successo aggiornando la documentazione, assicurando la disponibilità di attrezzature e effettuando controlli standard prima delle attività batch. - Fornire guida e formazione on-the-job ai colleghi su nuovi processi, pratiche di sicurezza e buoni comportamenti di documentazione, aiutando a promuovere una cultura di apprendimento e sviluppo di team. - Supportare la risoluzione dei problemi e risolvere i problemi con gli esperti di ingegneria, qualità e tecnica per mantenere le operazioni in esecuzione senza intoppi e implementare miglioramenti efficaci. - Utilizzare strumenti pratici di miglioramento continuo per semplificare i processi, migliorare l'efficienza e rafforzare le prestazioni complessive delle attrezzature e dei flussi di lavoro. - Rappresentare l'area di produzione con fiducia durante i controlli, i trasferimenti tecnici e le campagne di produzione anticipate legate alle nuove tecnologie introdotte. ## Requisiti - Corso di laurea in un campo scientifico o ingegneristico—o equivalente esperienza rilevante e apprendimento acquisito attraverso ruoli del settore. - Esperienza di lavoro nella produzione di GMP, nelle operazioni farmaceutiche, nelle biotecnologie o in un ambiente regolamentato simile. - Capacità di contribuire alle attività di introduzione di attrezzature e processi, con familiarità in settori come FAT/SAT e passi di qualificazione considerati vantaggiosi. - Competenze di comunicazione chiare e di supporto, con la capacità di formare e collaborare con colleghi attraverso funzioni e livelli. - Un approccio pratico alla risoluzione dei problemi, utilizzando metodi strutturati per identificare le cause della radice e migliorare l'azionamento. - Una mentalità proattiva e organizzata, con la capacità di bilanciare più attività mantenendo un impegno per la sicurezza, la qualità e il miglioramento continuo. # Cosa# offrendo - Una carriera agile e una cultura di lavoro dinamica, con l'opportunità di lavorare su un incarico internazionale - Un ambiente altamente collaborativo che favorisce il lavoro di squadra e l'inclusione - Una gamma di programmi di sviluppo professionale per crescere le vostre competenze e carriera - Un luogo di lavoro etico in cui i compiti sono svolti secondo le procedure pertinenti - L'elenco completo dei nostri benefici globali è disponibile anche su https://www.lonza.com/careers/benefits. ## Azienda At Lonza, la nostra gente Ã ̈ la nostra massima forza. Con 30+ siti in cinque continenti, i nostri team collegati a livello globale lavorano insieme ogni giorno per produrre i farmaci di domani. I nostri valori fondamentali di collaborazione, responsabilità, eccellenza, passione e integrità riflettono chi siamo e come lavoriamo insieme. Le idee di tutti, grandi o piccole, hanno il potenziale di migliorare milioni di vite, e questo è il tipo di lavoro di cui vogliamo che tu faccia parte. L'innovazione prospera quando le persone di tutti gli sfondi portano le loro prospettive uniche al tavolo. A Lonza, apprezziamo la diversità e ci impegniamo a creare un ambiente inclusivo per tutti i dipendenti. Se siete pronti ad aiutare a trasformare le idee innovative dei nostri clienti in terapie valide, non vediamo l'ora di accogliervi a bordo. ## Contatto Pronto a plasmare il futuro delle scienze della vita? Applichi subito. Lonza è leader globale nella produzione di farmaci e biotecnologie. L'azienda gestisce importanti stabilimenti produttivi a Visp (Valais), Basilea e Stein (Aargau), Svizzera.
descriptionByLocale.en: After an onboarding period in Visp, Switzerland, the position will be based in the U.S. (East or Mid-West Coast) to support the expansion of small molecules/bioconjugation capabilities. Relocation targeted in Q1 2027 and assistance is available for eligible candidates and their families, if needed. In this role, you will have a direct impact by optimizing daily GMP operations, improving process robustness, and ensuring safe, compliant, and timely production. You will also play a key role in capability expansion—supporting equipment introductions, authoring critical documentation, and resolving issues through data driven problem solving and cross functional collaboration. What you will get: An agile career and dynamic working culture, with the opportunity to work on an international assignment A highly collaborative environment that fosters teamwork and inclusion A range of professional development programs to grow your skills and career An ethical workplace where tasks are carried out in accordance with relevant procedures The full list of our global benefits can be also found on https://www.lonza.com/careers/benefits. What you will do: Lead the day-to-day execution of manufacturing activities in a GMP environment, helping ensure safe, reliable, and high-quality operations. Partner closely with cross-functional teams to support the introduction of new equipment and capabilities, including user requirements input and participation in key project milestones such as FAT and SAT. Prepare manufacturing areas for successful operations by updating documentation, ensuring equipment readiness, and performing standard checks before batch activities. Provide guidance and on-the-job training to colleagues on new processes, safety practices, and good documentation behaviors, helping foster a culture of learning and team development. Support troubleshooting and problem-solving with engineering, quality, and technical experts to keep operations running smoothly and implement effective improvements. Use practical continuous-improvement tools to simplify processes, enhance efficiency, and strengthen overall performance of equipment and workflows. Represent the manufacturing area confidently during audits, technical transfers, and early production campaigns linked to newly introduced technologies. What we are looking for: Bachelor’s degree in a scientific or engineering field—or equivalent relevant experience and learning gained through industry roles. Experience working in GMP manufacturing, pharmaceutical operations, biotechnology, or a similar regulated environment. Experience in bioconjugates and/or advanced synthesis (e.g., complex biologics, ADCs, or small molecule processes) Ability to contribute to equipment and process introduction activities, with familiarity in areas such as FAT/SAT and qualification steps considered beneficial. Clear and supportive communication skills, with the ability to train and collaborate with colleagues across functions and levels. A practical approach to problem-solving, using structured methods to identify root causes and drive improvements. A proactive, organized mindset, with the ability to balance multiple activities while maintaining a commitment to safety, quality, and continuous improvement. Travel: Willingness and ability to travel to EMEA and NA to meet business objectives About Lonza At Lonza, our people are our greatest strength. With 30+ sites across five continents, our globally connected teams work together every day to manufacture the medicines of tomorrow. Our core values of Collaboration, Accountability, Excellence, Passion and Integrity reflect who we are and how we work together. Everyone’s ideas, big or small, have the potential to improve millions of lives, and that’s the kind of work we want you to be part of. Innovation thrives when people from all backgrounds bring their unique perspectives to the table. At Lonza, we value diversity and are committed to creating an inclusive environment for all employees. If you are ready to help turn our customers’ breakthrough ideas into viable therapies, we look forward to welcoming you on board. Ready to shape the future of life sciences? Apply now. Lonza is a global leader in pharma and biotech manufacturing. The company operates major production facilities in Visp (Valais), Basel, and Stein (Aargau), Switzerland.
descriptionByLocale.de: Nach einer Onboarding-Periode in Visp, Schweiz, wird die Position in den USA (Ost- oder Mid-West-Küste) zur Unterstützung der Expansion kleiner Moleküle/Biokonjugation Fähigkeiten. Umzug in Q1 2027 und Unterstützung für förderfähige Kandidaten und ihre Familien, wenn erforderlich. Â Mansioni - Führen Sie die tägliche Durchführung von Produktionsaktivitäten in einer GMP-Umgebung und helfen, sichere, zuverlässige und qualitativ hochwertige Arbeiten zu gewährleisten. - Partner eng mit funktionsübergreifenden Teams zusammen, um die Einführung neuer Geräte und Fähigkeiten zu unterstützen, einschließlich der Eingabe und Teilnahme von Benutzeranforderungen an wichtige Projekt-Meilen wie FAT und SAT. - Vorbereitung der Fertigungsbereiche für den erfolgreichen Betrieb durch Aktualisierung der Dokumentation, Sicherstellung der Gerätebereitschaft und Durchführung von Standardkontrollen vor Batch-Aktivitäten. - Beratung und On-the-Job-Training an Kollegen über neue Prozesse, Sicherheitspraktiken und gute Dokumentations-Verhalten zur Förderung einer Kultur des Lernens und der Teamentwicklung. - Unterstützung der Fehlersuche und Problemlösung mit Ingenieur-, Qualitäts- und technischen Experten, um den Betrieb reibungslos laufen zu lassen und effektive Verbesserungen durchzuführen. - Verwenden Sie praktische kontinuierliche Verbesserungswerkzeuge, um Prozesse zu vereinfachen, die Effizienz zu erhöhen und die Gesamtleistung von Geräten und Workflows zu stärken. - Vertreten Sie den Fertigungsbereich zuversichtlich bei Audits, technischen Transfers und frühen Produktionskampagnen im Zusammenhang mit neu eingeführten Technologien. Requisiti - Bachelor-Abschluss in einem wissenschaftlichen oder technischen Bereich – oder gleichwertige relevante Erfahrung und Lernen durch Branchenrollen gewonnen. - Erleben Sie die Arbeit in der GMP-Herstellung, im pharmazeutischen Betrieb, in der Biotechnologie oder in einem ähnlichen geregelten Umfeld. - Fähigkeit, zu Ausrüstungs- und Prozesseinleitungsaktivitäten beizutragen, mit Vertrautheit in Bereichen wie FAT/SAT und Qualifizierungsschritte als vorteilhaft. - Klare und unterstützende Kommunikationsfähigkeiten, mit der Fähigkeit, mit Kollegen über Funktionen und Ebenen zu trainieren und zu kooperieren. - Ein praktischer Ansatz zur Problemlösung, mit strukturierten Methoden, um Wurzel- und Antriebsverbesserungen zu identifizieren. - Eine proaktive, organisierte Denkweise, mit der Fähigkeit, mehrere Aktivitäten auszugleichen und gleichzeitig ein Engagement für Sicherheit, Qualität und kontinuierliche Verbesserung. Cosa offriamo - Eine agile Karriere und dynamische Arbeitskultur, mit der Möglichkeit, an einer internationalen Aufgabe zu arbeiten - Ein hochkollaboratives Umfeld, das Teamarbeit und Inklusion fördert - Eine Reihe von professionellen Entwicklungsprogrammen, um Ihre Fähigkeiten und Karriere zu wachsen - Ein ethischer Arbeitsplatz, an dem Aufgaben nach einschlägigen Verfahren durchgeführt werden - Die vollständige Liste unserer weltweiten Vorteile finden Sie auch unter https://www.lonza.com/careers/benefits. Azienda At Lonza, unsere Leute sind unsere größte Stärke. Mit 30+ Standorten auf fünf Kontinenten arbeiten unsere weltweit vernetzten Teams jeden Tag zusammen, um die Medikamente von morgen zu fertigen. Unsere Kernwerte von Zusammenarbeit, Rechenschaftspflicht, Exzellenz, Leidenschaft und Integrität reflektieren, wer wir sind und wie wir zusammenarbeiten. Alle Ideen, groß oder klein, haben das Potenzial, Millionen von Leben zu verbessern, und das ist die Art von Arbeit, die wir wollen, dass Sie Teil. Innovation blüht, wenn Menschen aus allen Hintergründen ihre einzigartigen Perspektiven auf den Tisch bringen. Bei Lonza schätzen wir Diversität und sind verpflichtet, ein inklusives Umfeld für alle Mitarbeiter zu schaffen. Wenn Sie dazu bereit sind, die bahnbrechenden Ideen unserer Kunden in tragfähige Therapien zu verwandeln, freuen wir uns darauf, Sie an Bord zu begrüßen. Sind Sie bereit, die Zukunft der Lebenswissenschaften zu gestalten? Bewerben Sie sich. Lonza ist weltweit führend in der Pharma- und Biotech-Produktion. Das Unternehmen betreibt große Produktionsstätten in Visp (Valais), Basel und Stein (Aargau), Schweiz.
descriptionByLocale.fr: Descrizione Après une période d'embarquement à Viège, en Suisse, la position sera basée aux États-Unis (Est ou Mid-West Coast) pour soutenir l'expansion de petites molécules/capacités de bioconjugaison. La réinstallation visée au premier trimestre 2027 et l'aide sont disponibles pour les candidats admissibles et leurs familles, au besoin. ## Mansioni - Diriger l'exécution quotidienne des activités de fabrication dans un environnement de BPF, aidant à assurer des opérations sûres, fiables et de haute qualité. - Collaborer étroitement avec des équipes interfonctionnelles pour appuyer l'introduction de nouveaux équipements et capacités, y compris les besoins des utilisateurs et la participation à des étapes clés du projet telles que la FAT et la SAT. - Préparer les zones de fabrication pour des opérations réussies en mettant à jour la documentation, en veillant à ce que l'équipement soit prêt et en effectuant des vérifications normalisées avant les activités des lots. - Fournir des conseils et une formation en cours d'emploi aux collègues sur les nouveaux processus, les pratiques de sécurité et les bons comportements en matière de documentation, contribuant ainsi à favoriser une culture d'apprentissage et de perfectionnement en équipe. - Soutenir le dépannage et la résolution de problèmes avec des experts techniques, de qualité et d'ingénierie pour assurer le bon fonctionnement des opérations et mettre en œuvre des améliorations efficaces. - Utiliser des outils pratiques d'amélioration continue pour simplifier les processus, améliorer l'efficacité et renforcer la performance globale des équipements et des workflows. - Représenter le secteur manufacturier avec confiance lors des audits, des transferts techniques et des campagnes de production précoce liées aux nouvelles technologies. ## Requisiti - Baccalauréat dans un domaine scientifique ou d'ingénierie – ou l'expérience pertinente équivalente et l'apprentissage acquis grâce aux rôles de l'industrie. - Expérience dans la fabrication de BPF, les opérations pharmaceutiques, la biotechnologie ou un environnement réglementé semblable. - Capacité de contribuer aux activités d'introduction de l'équipement et des processus, avec connaissance dans des domaines tels que les TAF/SAT et les étapes de qualification jugées bénéfiques. - Compétences claires et favorables en communication, avec la capacité de former et de collaborer avec des collègues à tous les niveaux et fonctions. - Une approche pratique de la résolution des problèmes, utilisant des méthodes structurées pour identifier les causes profondes et conduire à des améliorations. - Un état d'esprit proactif et organisé, capable d'équilibrer plusieurs activités tout en maintenant un engagement en matière de sécurité, de qualité et d'amélioration continue. ## Cosa offriamo - Une carrière agile et une culture de travail dynamique, avec l'occasion de travailler sur une affectation internationale - Un environnement hautement collaboratif qui favorise le travail d'équipe et l'inclusion - Une gamme de programmes de perfectionnement professionnel pour développer vos compétences et votre carrière - Un lieu de travail éthique où les tâches sont exécutées conformément aux procédures pertinentes - La liste complète de nos avantages globaux peut également être consultée à l'adresse https://www.lonza.com/careers/ benefits. Azienda Chez Lonza, notre peuple est notre plus grande force. Avec plus de 30 sites sur cinq continents, nos équipes connectées à l'échelle mondiale travaillent tous les jours pour fabriquer les médicaments de demain. Nos valeurs fondamentales de collaboration, de responsabilisation, d'excellence, de passion et d'intégrité reflètent qui nous sommes et comment nous travaillons ensemble. Les idées de tout le monde, grandes ou petites, ont le potentiel d'améliorer des millions de vies, et c'est le genre de travail auquel nous voulons que vous participiez. L'innovation prospère lorsque les gens de tous les horizons apportent leurs points de vue uniques à la table. Chez Lonza, nous apprécions la diversité et nous nous engageons à créer un environnement inclusif pour tous les employés. Si vous êtes prêt à aider à transformer nos clients en thérapies viables, nous sommes impatients de vous accueillir à bord. ## Contatto Prêt à façonner l'avenir des sciences de la vie? Demande maintenant. Lonza est un leader mondial dans la fabrication de produits pharmaceutiques et de biotechnologies. La société exploite d'importantes installations de production à Viège (Valais), Bâle et Stein (Argovie), Suisse.

jobs.title: Security Specialist (m/w/d)
jobs.description:
Lonza ist heute ein weltweit führendes Unternehmen im Bereich Life Sciences, welches auf drei Kontinenten tätig ist. Wir sind in der Wissenschaft tätig, jedoch gibt es keine Zauberformel dafür, wie wir die Arbeit angehen. Unsere wichtigste wissenschaftliche Lösung sind talentierte Mitarbeitende, die zusammenarbeiten und Ideen entwickeln, die anderen Unternehmen helfen, Menschen zu helfen. Im Gegenzug steuern unsere Mitarbeitenden ihre Karriere selbstständig. Denn ihre Ideen, ob gross und klein, verbessern die Welt. Und das ist die Art von Arbeit, an der wir Teil haben möchten.

Bei Lonza am Standort Visp ein/e Security Specialist gesucht. Haben Sie Lust auf eine abwechslungsreiche Stelle? Dann bewerben Sie sich heute!

Aufgaben:
Als repräsentative Visitenkarte unserer Auftraggeber (Arxada / DSM usw.) übernehmen Sie die freundliche Begrüssung von Kunden, Gästen und Mitarbeitenden, um eine positive Atmosphäre zu schaffen.
Erfassen und Triagieren der eingehenden Alarme und Treffen der notwendigen Dispositionen.
Überwachung der Gebäude- / Arealsicherheit mithilfe des Kamerasystems und den vorhandenen Sicherheits- / Überwachungssysteme.
Führung des Ereignisjournals und Erstellen von Einsatzrapporten.
Kategorie- und zeitgerechte Erstellung der Badges sowie deren Aktualisierung im Work Day, Zutrittskontrolle und Ausweiserstellsystem.
Unterstützung des Krisenstabes im Ereignisfall.
Bewachung Perimeter.

Anforderungsprofil:
Abgeschlossene Berufsausbildung.
Ausbildung im Security Bereich (Security-Coordinator CFPA-E oder gleichwertig).
Erfahrung im Sicherheitsbereich respektive Hintergrund im Sicherheitsdienst (Polizei, Zoll, Grenzwache, Militär, Securitas).
Sehr gute Deutschkenntnisse in Wort und Schrift, gute Kenntnisse in Englisch und Französisch.
Werkskentnisse von Vorteil.
Bereitschaft für Schichtarbeit.
Zuverlässige, flexible Person mit hoher Einsatzbereitschaft und Teamfähigkeit.

Lonza’s Produkte und Services wirken sich täglich positiv auf Millionen von Menschen aus. Für uns stellt dies nicht nur eine grosse Ehre, sondern auch eine grosse Verantwortung, dar. Wie wir unsere Geschäftsergebnisse erreichen ist für uns genauso wichtig wie unsere Erfolge selbst. Bei Lonza wird Respekt grossgeschrieben und wir schützen sowohl unsere Mitarbeiter als auch unsere Umwelt. Erfolg für uns bedeutet moralisch vertretbarer Fortschritt.

Menschen werden Teil der Lonza, um herausfordernde und komplexe Problemstellungen durch kreative und innovative Ansätze im Bereich Life Sciences zu lösen. Im Gegenzug bieten wir die Zufriedenheit, die mit der Verbesserung des Lebens auf der ganzen Welt einhergeht. Die Zufriedenheit, die damit einhergeht, einen sinnvollen Unterschied zu machen.

Lonza is a global leader in pharma and biotech manufacturing. The company operates major production facilities in Visp (Valais), Basel, and Stein (Aargau), Switzerland.

descriptionByLocale.it: Lonza Lonza è leader mondiale nelle scienze della vita, che opera in tre continenti. Siamo attivi nella scienza, ma non ci sono formule magiche per come lavoriamo. La nostra soluzione scientifica più importante sono dipendenti di talento che lavorano insieme e sviluppano idee che aiutano altre aziende ad aiutare le persone. In cambio, i nostri dipendenti gestiscono la loro carriera in modo indipendente. Perché le loro idee, grandi e piccole, migliorano il mondo. E questo è il tipo di lavoro che vogliamo condividere. ## Il lavoro a Lonza a Visp sta cercando uno specialista di sicurezza. Vuoi un posto vario? Allora applica oggi! ## Mansioni - # Come un biglietto da visita rappresentativo dei nostri clienti (Arxada / DSM, ecc.) prendono il controllo dell'accoglienza amichevole di clienti, ospiti e dipendenti per creare un'atmosfera positiva. - Catturare e tritare gli allarmi in arrivo e soddisfare le disposizioni necessarie. - Monitoraggio della sicurezza dell'edificio/area utilizzando il sistema della telecamera e i sistemi di monitoraggio/sicurezza esistenti. - Guidare la rivista dell'evento e creare report delle applicazioni. - Classifica e creazione tempestiva dei distintivi così come il loro aggiornamento nella giornata di lavoro, controllo di accesso e sistema di identificazione. - Sostegno alla squadra di crisi. - Controllo del perimetro. ## Requisiti - Formazione professionale completa. - Formazione in sicurezza (coordinatore di sicurezza CFPA-E o equivalente). - Esperienza nel campo della sicurezza o sottofondo nel servizio di sicurezza (polizia, dogana, guardia di frontiera, militare, Securitas). - Molto buona conoscenza tedesca in parola e scrittura, buona conoscenza in inglese e francese. - Scene di lavoro di vantaggio. - Pronto per il lavoro a turni. - Persona affidabile e flessibile con alto impegno e capacità di squadra. I prodotti e i servizi di Cosa# offrono un impatto positivo su milioni di persone ogni giorno. Per noi questo non è solo un grande onore, ma anche una grande responsabilità. Come raggiungere i nostri risultati aziendali è altrettanto importante per noi come i nostri successi stessi. A Lonza viene dato rispetto e proteggiamo sia i nostri dipendenti che il nostro ambiente. Il successo per noi significa progresso moralmente ragionevole. Le persone diventano parte della Lonza per risolvere problemi impegnativi e complessi attraverso approcci creativi e innovativi alle scienze della vita. In cambio, offriamo la soddisfazione associata al miglioramento della vita in tutto il mondo. La soddisfazione associata a fare una differenza significativa. ## Contatto Lonza è leader globale nella produzione di farmaci e biotecnologie. L'azienda gestisce importanti stabilimenti produttivi a Visp (Valais), Basilea e Stein (Aargau), Svizzera.
descriptionByLocale.en: Lonza Lonza is a world leader in life sciences, which operates on three continents. We are active in science, but there are no magic formulas for how we work. Our most important scientific solution are talented employees who work together and develop ideas that help other companies to help people. In return, our employees manage their career independently. Because their ideas, whether big and small, improve the world. And this is the kind of work we want to share. ## The job At Lonza at Visp is looking for a/e Security Specialist. Do you want a varied place? Then apply today! ## Mansioni - As a representative business card of our clients (Arxada / DSM, etc.) take over the friendly welcome of customers, guests and employees to create a positive atmosphere. - Capture and triate the incoming alarms and meet the necessary dispositions. - Monitoring the building/area security using the camera system and the existing security/monitoring systems. - Guide the event journal and create application reports. - Class and timely creation of the badges as well as their updating in the Work Day, access control and identification system. - Support of the crisis team in the event. - Guarding perimeter. ## Requisiti - Complete vocational training. - Training in security (security coordinator CFPA-E or equivalent). - Experience in the security field or background in security service (police, customs, border guard, military, Securitas). - Very good German knowledge in word and writing, good knowledge in English and French. - Work scenes of advantage. - Ready for shiftwork. - Reliable, flexible person with high commitment and team capacity. ## Cosa offriamo Lonza’s products and services have a positive impact on millions of people every day. For us this is not only a great honour, but also a great responsibility. As we achieve our business results is just as important to us as our successes themselves. At Lonza, respect is given and we protect both our employees and our environment. Success for us means morally reasonable progress. People become part of the Lonza to solve challenging and complex problems through creative and innovative approaches to life sciences. In return, we offer the satisfaction associated with the improvement of life around the world. The satisfaction associated with making a meaningful difference. ## Contatto Lonza is a global leader in pharma and biotech manufacturing. The company operates major production facilities in Visp (Valais), Basel, and Stein (Aargau), Switzerland.

Bewachung Perimeter. Anforderungsprofil: Abgeschlossene Berufsausbildung. Ausbildung im Security Bereich (Security-Coordinator CFPA-E oder gleichwertig). Erfahrung im Sicherheitsbereich respektive Hintergrund im Sicherheitsdienst (Polizei, Zoll, Grenzwache, Militär, Securitas). Sehr gute Deutschkenntnisse in Wort und Schrift, gute Kenntnisse in Englisch und Französisch. Werkskentnisse von Vorteil. Bereitschaft für Schichtarbeit. Zuverlässige, flexible Person mit hoher Einsatzbereitschaft und Teamfähigkeit. Lonza’s Produkte und Services wirken sich täglich positiv auf Millionen von Menschen aus. Für uns stellt dies nicht nur eine grosse Ehre, sondern auch eine grosse Verantwortung, dar. Wie wir unsere Geschäftsergebnisse erreichen ist für uns genauso wichtig wie unsere Erfolge selbst. Bei Lonza wird Respekt grossgeschrieben und wir schützen sowohl unsere Mitarbeiter als auch unsere Umwelt. Erfolg für uns bedeutet moralisch vertretbarer Fortschritt. Menschen werden Teil der Lonza, um herausfordernde und komplexe Problemstellungen durch kreative und innovative Ansätze im Bereich Life Sciences zu lösen. Im Gegenzug bieten wir die Zufriedenheit, die mit der Verbesserung des Lebens auf der ganzen Welt einhergeht. Die Zufriedenheit, die damit einhergeht, einen sinnvollen Unterschied zu machen. Lonza is a global leader in pharma and biotech manufacturing. The company operates major production facilities in Visp (Valais), Basel, and Stein (Aargau), Switzerland.
descriptionByLocale.fr: Lonza Lonza est un leader mondial des sciences de la vie, qui opère sur trois continents. Nous sommes actifs dans la science, mais il n'y a pas de formules magiques pour notre fonctionnement. Notre solution scientifique la plus importante est les employés talentueux qui travaillent ensemble et développent des idées qui aident d'autres entreprises à aider les gens. En retour, nos employés gèrent leur carrière de façon indépendante. Parce que leurs idées, grandes et petites, améliorent le monde. Et c'est le genre de travail que nous voulons partager. ## Le travail Chez Lonza à Viège est à la recherche d'un/e Spécialiste de la sécurité. Tu veux un endroit varié ? Alors appliquez aujourd'hui ! - Oui. En tant que carte d'affaires représentative de nos clients (Arxada / DSM, etc.), prendre en charge l'accueil amical des clients, des invités et des employés pour créer une atmosphère positive. - Capturer et trier les alarmes entrantes et répondre aux dispositions nécessaires. - Surveiller la sécurité du bâtiment/de la zone en utilisant le système de caméras et les systèmes de sécurité/de surveillance existants. - Guidez le journal de l'événement et créez des rapports d'application. - La création en classe et en temps opportun des badges ainsi que leur mise à jour lors de la journée de travail, le contrôle d'accès et le système d'identification. - Soutien de l'équipe de crise dans l'événement. - Le périmètre de garde. ## Requisiti - Formation professionnelle complète. - Formation en sécurité (coordonnateur de la sécurité CFPA-E ou équivalent). - Expérience dans le domaine de la sécurité ou dans les services de sécurité (police, douane, garde frontière, militaire, Securitas). - Très bonne connaissance allemande de la parole et de l'écriture, bonne connaissance en anglais et en français. - Des scènes d'avantage. - Prêt pour le travail posté. - Une personne fiable et flexible avec un engagement élevé et une capacité d'équipe. Les produits et services de Cosa offriamo Lonza ont un impact positif sur des millions de personnes chaque jour. Pour nous, ce n'est pas seulement un grand honneur, mais aussi une grande responsabilité. À mesure que nous atteignons nos résultats commerciaux, il est tout aussi important pour nous que nos succès eux-mêmes. À Lonza, le respect est donné et nous protégeons nos employés et notre environnement. Le succès pour nous signifie un progrès moralement raisonnable. Les gens font partie de la Lonza pour résoudre des problèmes complexes et difficiles grâce à des approches créatives et novatrices des sciences de la vie. En retour, nous offrons la satisfaction associée à l'amélioration de la vie dans le monde entier. La satisfaction associée à faire une différence significative. ## Contatto Lonza est un leader mondial dans la fabrication de produits pharmaceutiques et de biotechnologies. La société exploite d'importantes installations de production à Viège (Valais), Bâle et Stein (Argovie), Suisse.

jobs.title: Senior Scientist, Equipment Qualification (m/f/d) 80-100%
jobs.description:
The actual location of this job is in Stein, AG, Switzerland. Relocation assistance is available for eligible candidates and their families, if needed.

Lead the GMP-compliant qualification and re‑qualification of a great variety of analytical instruments and software in our QC laboratory. At our growing site, this position provides exceptional opportunities to learn, contribute, and make a meaningful impact. Check out your career in Quality Control!

What you will get:
An agile career and a dynamic work culture.

An inclusive and ethical workplace.

Compensation programs that recognize high performance.

In addition to a competitive salary, you can expect numerous lifestyle, family, and leisure benefits. Our full list of tailored local benefits can be found below.
Benefits in Stein: https://bit.ly/42DlBDk 

What you will do:
Lead and manage the initial qualification and re-qualification of analytical equipment and software in our QC laboratory (HPLC, CE, refrigerators etc.)

Execution of performance qualification and act as a trainer for defined equipment and related processes

Author, review and approve documentation (qualification plans and reports; documents from service providers) related to laboratory systems as required

Ensure compliance of laboratory systems according to cGMP and current standard processes and regulatory guidelines as well as respective SOP`s.

Oversight and guidance for proper maintenance and qualified state of equipment; Performing period review of qualification/calibration of analytical equipment and act as system owner for defined Equipment

Participate as Subject Matter Expert in audits and inspections

What we are looking for:
Solid work experience in the GMP-controlled Quality Control laboratory is a must

Hands-on experience in qualification of analytical instruments is a must

Apprenticeship or academical degree in Analytical Chemistry, Biochemistry, Pharmaceutical Sciences or related field

Broad experience in analytical methodologies like e.g. HPLC, ICief, UV-VIS, Elisa, CE-SDS is an advantage. Knowledge of lab quality systems like LIMS, Tiamo or Empower/Chromeleon is a plus

Fluency in written and spoken English is a must, Language skills in German is a plus

You enjoy working independently and in a dynamic & fast-pacing project environment

About Lonza
At Lonza, our people are our greatest strength. With 30+ sites across five continents, our globally connected teams work together every day to manufacture the medicines of tomorrow. Our core values of Collaboration, Accountability, Excellence, Passion and Integrity reflect who we are and how we work together. Everyone’s ideas, big or small, have the potential to improve millions of lives, and that’s the kind of work we want you to be part of.
Innovation thrives when people from all backgrounds bring their unique perspectives to the table. At Lonza, we value diversity and are committed to creating an inclusive environment for all employees. If you’re ready to help turn our customers’ breakthrough ideas into viable therapies, we look forward to welcoming you on board.
Ready to shape the future of life sciences? Apply now.

Lonza is a global leader in pharma and biotech manufacturing. The company operates major production facilities in Visp (Valais), Basel, and Stein (Aargau), Switzerland.

descriptionByLocale.fr: Descrizione L'emplacement réel de ce travail est à Stein, AG, Suisse. documents des fournisseurs de services) relative aux systèmes de laboratoire selon les besoins - Assurer la conformité des systèmes de laboratoire selon le GMPc et les processus standards et directives réglementaires en vigueur ainsi que les PON respectifs. - Surveillance et orientation pour l'entretien adéquat et l'état qualifié de l'équipement; Réalisation de l'examen de la période de qualification/étalonnage de l'équipement analytique et agir comme propriétaire du système pour un équipement défini - Participer en tant qu'expert en matière de vérification et d'inspection ## Requisiti - Une solide expérience de travail dans le laboratoire de contrôle de la qualité contrôlé par les BPF est un must - Expérience pratique en matière de qualification d'instruments d'analyse est un must - Apprenticeship ou diplôme universitaire en chimie analytique, biochimie, sciences pharmaceutiques ou domaine connexe - Grande expérience dans les méthodologies analytiques comme HPLC, ICief, UV-VIS, Elisa, CE-SDS est un avantage - Connaissance des systèmes de qualité de laboratoire comme LIMS, Tiamo ou Empower/Chromeleon est un plus - La fluidité en anglais écrit et parlé est un must - Les compétences linguistiques en allemand sont un plus - Vous aimez travailler indépendamment et dans un environnement de projet dynamique et rapide ## Cosa offriamo - Une carrière agile et une culture de travail dynamique - Un milieu de travail inclusif et éthique - Programmes de rémunération qui reconnaissent la performance élevée - En plus d'un salaire concurrentiel, vous pouvez vous attendre à de nombreux avantages liés au mode de vie, à la famille et aux loisirs - Notre liste complète des avantages locaux sur mesure se trouve ci-dessous : https://bit.ly/42DlBDk ## Contatto Prêt à façonner l'avenir des sciences de la vie? jobs.title: Group Leader Analytical Development (Small Molecules) 80-100% (m/f/d)

titleByLocale.it: Leader del Gruppo Sviluppo analitico (molecole minori) 80-100% (m/f/d)

jobs.description:
The actual location of this job is in Visp, Switzerland. Relocation assistance is available for eligible candidates and their families, if needed.

In this senior leadership role, you will lead an interdisciplinary Analytical Development team responsible for the implementation, development,  optimization, and validation of analytical methods for  small‑molecule products in a GMP‑regulated environment. In addition, the team provides analytical expertise and measurement support to process development and manufacturing teams, advising on analytical techniques, data interpretation, and method selection.
You will ensure that people, capabilities, and assets consistently deliver high‑quality analytical services, particularly chromatography techniques to internal and external customers, in line with agreed timelines, cost targets, and safety standards.

What you will get
An agile career and a dynamic work culture.

An inclusive and ethical workplace.

Compensation programs that recognize high performance.

In addition to a competitive salary, you can expect numerous lifestyle, family, and leisure benefits. Our full list of tailored local benefits can be found below.
Benefits in Visp: https://bit.ly/3wjkoFi 

What you will do:

Provide scientific and disciplinary leadership for a team of approximately

30 development scientists, project leaders, and laboratory technicians

Coordinates and optimizes the allocation of resources (e.g. personnel, equipment, laboratories, etc) to meet project needs and customer expectations. 

Oversee the development, validation, transfer of analytical methods, and support of process development and manufacturing teams with a strong focus on chromatographic techniques

Ensure full compliance with GMP, quality standards, and safety regulations across all development and production support activities

Manage project portfolios, budgets, timelines, and resource allocation to meet customer and business objectives

Act as a technical expert and key interface for internal stakeholders and external customers, including participation in audits and inspections

Drive continuous improvement, innovation, and operational excellence in analytical and chemical development processes

What we are looking for:

PhD or MSc in Chemistry, Analytical Chemistry, Pharmacy, or Chemical Engineering

More than 10 years of industrial experience in chemical and/or analytical development within a GMP environment

Strong expertise in chromatographic methods (e.g. HPLC/UPLC, LC‑MS, GC) applied to process understanding and optimization

You demonstrate creative problem‑solving, active listening, and strong communication skills, enabling effective cross‑functional collaboration and interdisciplinary teamwork.

Demonstrated leadership experience managing and developing multidisciplinary teams in an industrial setting

Solid understanding of business processes, cost control, and customer‑focused project execution

Fluency in English; German proficiency or strong willingness to learn German

About Lonza

At Lonza, our people are our greatest strength. With 30+ sites across five continents, our globally connected teams work together every day to manufacture the medicines of tomorrow. Our core values of Collaboration, Accountability, Excellence, Passion and Integrity reflect who we are and how we work together. Everyone’s ideas, big or small, have the potential to improve millions of lives, and that’s the kind of work we want you to be part of.

Innovation thrives when people from all backgrounds bring their unique perspectives to the table. At Lonza, we value diversity and are committed to creating an inclusive environment for all employees. If you’re ready to help turn our customers’ breakthrough ideas into viable therapies, we look forward to welcoming you on board.

Ready to shape the future of life sciences? 
Apply now.

Lonza is a global leader in pharma and biotech manufacturing. The company operates major production facilities in Visp (Valais), Basel, and Stein (Aargau), Switzerland.

jobs.title: Scientist QC Microbiology 80-100%
jobs.description:
The actual location of this job is in Stein, AG, Switzerland. Relocation assistance is available for eligible candidates and their families, if needed.

Join us as Scientist in our QC Microbiology department and become part of an exciting growth project for a new sterile drug product facility! Check out your career at Drug Product Services (DPS)

What you will get:
An agile career and a dynamic work culture.

An inclusive and ethical workplace.

Compensation programs that recognize high performance.

In addition to a competitive salary, you can expect numerous lifestyle, family, and leisure benefits. Our full list of tailored local benefits can be found below.
Benefits in Stein: https://bit.ly/42DlBDk 

What you’ll do:
Routine execution and coordination of microbiological testing including environmental monitoring, bacterial endotoxins-, bioburden- and sterility testing etc.

Adhere to GMP regulations to ensure flawless operations

Perform, develop, establish and improve microbiological testing procedures, e.g. method suitability testing and provide training for test methods

Support microbiology-related projects, such as implementation of new methods

Ensure appropriate maintenance of equipment, safety and health protection following laboratory and safety instructions and all cGMP regulations

Train analytical methods and/or corresponding instruments

What we’re looking for:
Education as Lab Technician, Bachelor or equivalent in Microbiology or Biology, Pharmaceutical Sciences or related fields

Working experience in microbiological testing, preferably in pharmaceutical industry working under cGMP Regulations

Sound working knowledge in all (and in-depth knowledge in at least one) relevant microbiological test types (environmental monitoring, endotoxin, bioburden and sterility testing)

Knowledge with automated systems (e.g. Growth direct, Pyrotec Pro) is an asset

Excellent communication skills

Fluency in written and spoken English, German is an asset

About Lonza
At Lonza, our people are our greatest strength. With 30+ sites across five continents, our globally connected teams work together every day to manufacture the medicines of tomorrow. Our core values of Collaboration, Accountability, Excellence, Passion and Integrity reflect who we are and how we work together. Everyone’s ideas, big or small, have the potential to improve millions of lives, and that’s the kind of work we want you to be part of.
Innovation thrives when people from all backgrounds bring their unique perspectives to the table. At Lonza, we value diversity and are committed to creating an inclusive environment for all employees. If you’re ready to help turn our customers’ breakthrough ideas into viable therapies, we look forward to welcoming you on board.
Ready to shape the future of life sciences? Apply now.

Lonza is a global leader in pharma and biotech manufacturing. The company operates major production facilities in Visp (Valais), Basel, and Stein (Aargau), Switzerland.

jobs.title: Head Visual Inspection 80-100%
jobs.description:
The actual location of this job is in Stein, AG, Switzerland. Relocation assistance is available for eligible candidates and their families, if needed.

In this role you will have the opportunity to lead a business‑critical visual inspection and container closure integrity testing team at the heart of Drug Product Services. With full technical ownership across automated, semi‑automated, and manual visual inspection processes, you will partner closely with customers, health authorities, and cross‑functional teams to deliver safe, compliant, and high‑quality outcomes. You will lead and develop a fast‑growing expert team, driving innovation and successfully scaling next‑generation inspection technologies as part of an exciting growth project.
Check out Your Career in Drug Product  Services at Lonza !

What you will get:
An agile career and a dynamic work culture.

A highly collaborative environment that fosters teamwork and inclusion.

An inclusive and ethical workplace.

Compensation programs that recognize high performance.

In addition to a competitive salary, you can expect numerous lifestyle, family, and leisure benefits. Our full list of tailored local benefits can be found below.
Benefits in Stein: https://bit.ly/42DlBDk

What you will do:

Manage a team of a Vision Engineers, Process Experts and Operators with responsibility for all aspects of people leadership such as development, coaching, hiring and disciplinary topics. Maintain a culture of empowerment and innovation, trust, high performance and change readiness.

Technical system ownership: Provide scientific leadership to the team and other functions to ensure visual inspection processes are executed timely, cost effective and with the highest quality. Proactively anticipate, evaluate and resolve technical challenges and enables innovation.

Responsible for operative takeover of the automated, semi-automated,  manual visual inspection and  container closure integrity testing

Manage the customer interface in technical client visits and adapt technical capabilities in order to meet the customer requirements. Represent the facility during regulatory inspections and audits of clients and health authorities.

Accountable for safety, compliance and regulatory aspects. Preparation of risk analysis and all relevant procedures to ensure regulatory compliance and achieve business goals.

Support of the project team regarding complex topics by use of scientific and statistical knowledge.

Ensure and manage the interfaces between CAPEX team, Manufacturing, Plant Engineering, Automation, Support, QC and QA. Collaborate with functions in the project and across Drug Product Services, especially the existing drug product facilities)

Support the Senior Director Operational Lead of one of our largest growth project (Dolphin) in developing, prioritizing and achieving goals with respect to the project and overall DPS (Drug Product Services) goals. Ensure a good information exchange from the management level to the organization and the other way around through regularly team meetings and clear communication.

What we are looking for:

Extensive experience in visual inspection of parenteral drug product (specifically vials and syringes) in a GMP environment is a must. Broad experience with various different visual inspection systems such as manual, semi-automatic or AVI is an advantage.

Team leadership experience is a prerequisite

Experience with audits of health authorities

Fluency in English is a must (fluency in German is an advantage) with strong communication skills with stakeholders from various functions and levels.

About Lonza
At Lonza, our people are our greatest strength. With 30+ sites across five continents, our globally connected teams work together every day to manufacture the medicines of tomorrow. Our core values of Collaboration, Accountability, Excellence, Passion and Integrity reflect who we are and how we work together. Everyone’s ideas, big or small, have the potential to improve millions of lives, and that’s the kind of work we want you to be part of.

Innovation thrives when people from all backgrounds bring their unique perspectives to the table. At Lonza, we value diversity and are committed to creating an inclusive environment for all employees. If you’re ready to help turn our customers’ breakthrough ideas into viable therapies, we look forward to welcoming you on board.

Ready to shape the future of life sciences? 
Apply now.

Lonza is a global leader in pharma and biotech manufacturing. The company operates major production facilities in Visp (Valais), Basel, and Stein (Aargau), Switzerland.

L'innovazione prospera quando le persone di tutti gli sfondi portano le loro prospettive uniche al tavolo. A Lonza, apprezziamo la diversità e ci impegniamo a creare un ambiente inclusivo per tutti i dipendenti. Se siete pronti ad aiutare a trasformare le idee innovative dei nostri clienti in terapie valide, non vediamo l'ora di accogliervi a bordo. Lonza è leader globale nella produzione di farmaci e biotecnologie. L'azienda gestisce importanti stabilimenti produttivi a Visp (Valais), Basilea e Stein (Aargau), Svizzera.
descriptionByLocale.en: The actual location of this job is in Stein, AG, Switzerland. Relocation assistance is available for eligible candidates and their families, if needed. In this role you will have the opportunity to lead a business‑critical visual inspection and container closure integrity testing team at the heart of Drug Product Services. With full technical ownership across automated, semi‑automated, and manual visual inspection processes, you will partner closely with customers, health authorities, and cross‑functional teams to deliver safe, compliant, and high‑quality outcomes. You will lead and develop a fast‑growing expert team, driving innovation and successfully scaling next‑generation inspection technologies as part of an exciting growth project. Check out Your Career in Drug Product Services at Lonza ! What you will get: An agile career and a dynamic work culture. A highly collaborative environment that fosters teamwork and inclusion. An inclusive and ethical workplace. Compensation programs that recognize high performance. In addition to a competitive salary, you can expect numerous lifestyle, family, and leisure benefits. Our full list of tailored local benefits can be found below. Benefits in Stein: https://bit.ly/42DlBDk What you will do: Manage a team of a Vision Engineers, Process Experts and Operators with responsibility for all aspects of people leadership such as development, coaching, hiring and disciplinary topics. Maintain a culture of empowerment and innovation, trust, high performance and change readiness. Technical system ownership: Provide scientific leadership to the team and other functions to ensure visual inspection processes are executed timely, cost effective and with the highest quality. Proactively anticipate, evaluate and resolve technical challenges and enables innovation. Responsible for operative takeover of the automated, semi-automated, manual visual inspection and container closure integrity testing Manage the customer interface in technical client visits and adapt technical capabilities in order to meet the customer requirements. Represent the facility during regulatory inspections and audits of clients and health authorities. Accountable for safety, compliance and regulatory aspects. Preparation of risk analysis and all relevant procedures to ensure regulatory compliance and achieve business goals. Support of the project team regarding complex topics by use of scientific and statistical knowledge. Ensure and manage the interfaces between CAPEX team, Manufacturing, Plant Engineering, Automation, Support, QC and QA. Collaborate with functions in the project and across Drug Product Services, especially the existing drug product facilities) Support the Senior Director Operational Lead of one of our largest growth project (Dolphin) in developing, prioritizing and achieving goals with respect to the project and overall DPS (Drug Product Services) goals. Ensure a good information exchange from the management level to the organization and the other way around through regularly team meetings and clear communication. What we are looking for: Extensive experience in visual inspection of parenteral drug product (specifically vials and syringes) in a GMP environment is a must. Broad experience with various different visual inspection systems such as manual, semi-automatic or AVI is an advantage. Team leadership experience is a prerequisite Experience with audits of health authorities Fluency in English is a must (fluency in German is an advantage) with strong communication skills with stakeholders from various functions and levels. About Lonza At Lonza, our people are our greatest strength. With 30+ sites across five continents, our globally connected teams work together every day to manufacture the medicines of tomorrow. Our core values of Collaboration, Accountability, Excellence, Passion and Integrity reflect who we are and how we work together. Everyone’s ideas, big or small, have the potential to improve millions of lives, and that’s the kind of work we want you to be part of. Innovation thrives when people from all backgrounds bring their unique perspectives to the table. At Lonza, we value diversity and are committed to creating an inclusive environment for all employees. If you’re ready to help turn our customers’ breakthrough ideas into viable therapies, we look forward to welcoming you on board. Ready to shape the future of life sciences? Apply now. Lonza is a global leader in pharma and biotech manufacturing. The company operates major production facilities in Visp (Valais), Basel, and Stein (Aargau), Switzerland.

descriptionByLocale.fr: Descrizione L'emplacement réel de ce travail est à Stein, AG, Suisse. Une aide à la réinstallation est offerte aux candidats admissibles et à leur famille, au besoin. ## Mansioni - Gérer une équipe d'ingénieurs de la vision, d'experts en processus et d'opérateurs ayant la responsabilité de tous les aspects du leadership des personnes, comme le perfectionnement, l'encadrement, l'embauche et les sujets disciplinaires. Maintenir une culture d'autonomisation et d'innovation, de confiance, de haute performance et de préparation au changement. - Prise en charge du système technique : fournir un leadership scientifique à l'équipe et à d'autres fonctions pour s'assurer que les processus d'inspection visuelle sont exécutés en temps opportun, rentable et de la plus haute qualité. Prévoir, évaluer et résoudre les défis techniques de manière proactive et permettre l'innovation. - Responsable de la reprise opérationnelle de l'inspection visuelle manuelle automatisée, semi-automatisée et des tests d'intégrité de la fermeture des conteneurs - Gérer l'interface client dans les visites techniques du client et adapter les capacités techniques afin de répondre aux exigences du client. Représenter l'établissement pendant les inspections réglementaires et les vérifications des clients et des autorités sanitaires. - Responsable de la sécurité, de la conformité et des aspects réglementaires. Préparation d'une analyse des risques et de toutes les procédures pertinentes pour assurer la conformité à la réglementation et atteindre les objectifs opérationnels. - Appui de l ' équipe chargée du projet sur des sujets complexes grâce à des connaissances scientifiques et statistiques. - Assurer et gérer les interfaces entre l'équipe CAPEX, la fabrication, le génie végétal, l'automatisation, le soutien, le QC et l'AQ. Collaborer avec les fonctions du projet et de l'ensemble des Services des produits pharmaceutiques, en particulier les installations existantes des produits pharmaceutiques) - Appuyer le directeur principal responsable opérationnel de l'un de nos plus grands projets de croissance (Dauphin) dans l'élaboration, la hiérarchisation et l'atteinte des objectifs du projet et des objectifs généraux du DPS (Services des produits pharmaceutiques). Assurer un bon échange d'information entre la direction et l'organisation, et ce, par le biais de réunions d'équipe régulières et de communications claires. ## Requisiti - Une vaste expérience dans l'inspection visuelle du produit pharmaceutique parentéral (en particulier les flacons et les seringues) dans un environnement BPF est un must. - Une vaste expérience avec différents systèmes d'inspection visuelle tels que manuel, semi-automatique ou AVI est un avantage. - Oui. Notre liste complète des prestations locales sur mesure se trouve ci-dessous. - Avantages à Stein: https://bit.ly/42DlBDk ## Contatto Prêt à façonner l'avenir des sciences de la vie?
